<!-- Generated by ac:claude-md-rules-creator on YYYY-MM-DD. This comment is block-level HTML, stripped before injection: zero token cost for the model, visible when a human opens the file. -->

<!--
Path-scoped rule template for .claude/rules/<topic>.md.

Path: <project>/.claude/rules/<topic>.md (project) OR ~/.claude/rules/<topic>.md (user-global).

KEEP THE FRONTMATTER if you want path-scoping. DROP IT for an always-loaded topic file.
Use `paths:` (NOT `globs:`). Only `paths:` is recognized; other keys are silently ignored.
`paths: ['**']` is treated identically to no `paths:` (unconditional). Use real globs.
-->

---
paths:
  - "src/[area]/**/*.[ext]"
  - "tests/[area]/**/*.[ext]"
---

# [Topic Name]

[One-paragraph intro: what this rule covers and when it applies. Optional. Skip if rules are self-evident.]

- [Rule 1: specific, verifiable, concrete. "Validate every input with Zod schemas in src/server/schemas/." Not "use proper validation."]
- [Rule 2: name a file path or function the reader can point at.]
- [Rule 3: "Don't X. Do Y instead." Always pair prohibitions with the alternative.]
- [Rule 4: log requirements, error format, naming convention, etc.]

## [Optional subsection]

- [Sub-topic rules. Keep the whole file under 30 lines.]

<!--
Authoring checklist:
- [ ] Frontmatter uses `paths:` (not `globs:`). Drop frontmatter entirely if always-loaded.
- [ ] Each rule is specific enough to verify.
- [ ] No aspirations ("we aim for X").
- [ ] No prohibitions without alternatives ("don't X" -> "don't X; do Y instead").
- [ ] File under 30 lines total.
- [ ] One topic. If you find yourself adding subsections that aren't about this topic, split into a second file.
- [ ] Glob pattern actually matches the files where the rule should apply. Test mentally with a representative file path.
- [ ] No file-by-file tours, no standard-language conventions, no secrets.

Loading behavior reminder:
- WITH `paths:` -> loads on demand when Claude reads a file matching the glob via the Read tool. Edit/Write/MultiEdit do NOT trigger.
- Once loaded, persists in conversation context for the rest of the session.
- Compact summarizes path-scoped rules away. They reload on next matching file read.
- WITHOUT `paths:` -> loads at session start with the same priority as `.claude/CLAUDE.md`. Survives compact.
-->
